title: Dynamic sounds capture PPS boundaries
citation: Canzoneri, E., Magosso, E., & Serino, A. (2012). Dynamic sounds capture the boundaries of peripersonal space representation in humans. PLoS ONE, 7(9), e44306.
notes: Canonical dynamic audio-tactile PPS task. The preload now follows the original paper's accessible method values where the current scheduler can express them. Exact replication still needs direction-coupled tactile-only T0/T6 baseline trials, which the current Trial Assembler cannot yet encode as a distinct baseline subtype.

reference_parameters.baseline_subtype: tactile-only during silence before or after the dynamic sound
reference_parameters.response_rule: vocal response to tactile target, ignore sound
reference_parameters.analysis_outlier_rule: trim RTs exceeding 2 SD from participant/condition mean
implementation_gaps: exact two-speaker SoundForge exponential gain files

provenance.source: PLOS ONE Methods report T1-T5 as 300, 800, 1500, 2200, and 2700 ms from sound onset.
PLOS ONE Methods report 112 tactile-target trials and 76 auditory-only catch trials split across two blocks.
PLOS ONE Methods report 3000 ms pink noise with near/far two-speaker exponential intensity manipulation.
